name: Credit Done Right
description_short: San Antonio-area credit repair firm founded in 2009 that disputes collections and charge-offs to help clients qualify for mortgages, auto loans, and other financing.
description_long:
Credit Done Right was founded in 2009 by Damon Green and Rhonda Green in the San Antonio, Texas area, built from the ground up to help credit-challenged consumers clear the barriers preventing them from obtaining home loans and other financing. After operating for over a decade, the company opened a physical office in December 2021 at 8940 Fourwinds Drive, Suite 201, in Windcrest, TX. The firm employs roughly 20 data processors and customer service professionals and serves clients across Texas through a related entity (creditdonerightinc.com). No third-party professional certifications — such as NFCC membership, HUD approval, or CDFI designation — have been publicly verified for this company.

The company's core offering is credit repair through aggressive bureau disputes targeting negative items, particularly collections and charge-offs. Their Silver package covers removal of up to 10 such items over a 3–4 month timeline. Higher-tier packages address up to 20 items, though full details aren't published online. Each client receives a dedicated team member and monthly progress updates. Beyond bureau disputes, the firm helps clients stop debt collector contact and prepares people to qualify for mortgages, auto loans, and specialty credit lines such as solar and pool financing. Pricing is not available online; a consultation is required for a custom quote.

Credit Done Right's most notable differentiator is a score guarantee — a commitment to bring clients to a specific target credit score within a defined timeframe. This is especially meaningful given their primary market: the company's referral pipeline runs through real estate agents, mortgage brokers, and banks who send pre-approved-but-credit-challenged buyers their way. That embedded trust from real estate professionals is something purely direct-to-consumer shops lack. With 17 years in business by 2026, they also have substantially more longevity than most local credit repair operations, which tend to come and go.

The firm's strengths are genuine: nearly two decades of experience, a score guarantee most competitors won't commit to, and a 4.3/5 Google rating from 125 reviews that suggests consistent client satisfaction. The limitations are equally real. Pricing is completely opaque — no figures appear anywhere on their site, making cost comparison impossible without a phone call. The company is not BBB-accredited and has no confirmed third-party certifications. Their money-back guarantee policy is not publicly documented; only the score-achievement guarantee is mentioned. For a consumer referred by a trusted realtor or lender, these gaps may feel minor. For someone searching independently, they are meaningful hurdles before even knowing what this firm charges.\n\nIn the broader ecosystem of credit repair services, consumers have multiple paths to improving their credit. Professional credit repair companies can dispute inaccurate items with all three bureaus, while credit monitoring services provide ongoing alerts about changes to your reports. For those building credit from scratch, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured approaches. Consumers dealing with overwhelming debt may benefit from debt consolidation loans to simplify payments, or credit counseling through nonprofit agencies for personalized budgeting guidance. Consumers who successfully repair their credit often find better rates on installment loans, secured credit cards, and other financial products.

pricing.guarantee_details: Score-achievement guarantee: clients are promised they will reach a specific target credit score within a defined timeframe. Refund or money-back terms are not publicly disclosed. Contact provider for full guarantee details.

tiers.name: Silver
features: Up to 10 collections and charge-offs disputed for removal
Dedicated team member assigned to your account
Monthly status updates throughout the process
Credit score improvement toward a target score

pros: Founded in 2009 — 17 years of credit repair experience, unusually long track record for a local Texas firm
Score guarantee: commits to achieving a specific target credit score within a defined timeframe, a promise most competitors avoid
Each client gets a dedicated team member, not a rotating call center representative
Strong referral pipeline from real estate agents, mortgage lenders, and banks signals established industry trust
4.3/5 Google rating from 125 reviews indicates consistent client satisfaction over time
Physical office in Windcrest, TX available for in-person consultations
Serves clients statewide across Texas, not limited to San Antonio metro area
cons: Zero pricing transparency — no costs published online, requiring a consultation before any comparison shopping is possible
Not BBB-accredited; current BBB rating is unconfirmed
No verified third-party certifications (not NFCC-affiliated, not HUD-approved, not CDFI-certified)
Money-back guarantee is not publicly documented — only a score-achievement guarantee is mentioned, with no stated refund terms
Small staff of ~20 may limit capacity and responsiveness during high-demand periods
best_for: Prospective homebuyers who don't yet qualify for a mortgage and were referred by their real estate agent or lender
Individuals with multiple collections or charge-offs seeking targeted removal over a 3–4 month period
Texas residents needing credit improvement to qualify for auto loans, solar financing, or pool loans
People experiencing active debt collector harassment who want professional intervention
diagnosis: Credit Done Right is best suited for referred clients — specifically prospective homebuyers or borrowers sent by a real estate agent, mortgage broker, or lender who already trusts the firm. Their score guarantee and mortgage-prep specialization make them a strong match for that narrow use case, backed by 17 years of operational history. The primary caveat for independent searchers is complete pricing opacity: without a published fee structure, consumers cannot evaluate cost-competitiveness before committing to a consultation.
services: Negative item disputes targeting collections and charge-offs
Credit report restoration through bureau dispute process
Credit score improvement toward a client-specific target score
Mortgage qualification preparation and credit remediation
Pool loan and line of credit qualification support
Debt collector interference resolution and stoppage
Dedicated team member assignment per client account
Monthly status updates and progress reporting
Tiered service packages with defined item-removal caps (Silver: up to 10 items; higher tiers: up to 20 items)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
